title: Tissue extraction from whole caterpillars
author: James JN Kitson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bz62p9ge
materials: Wash Buffer 1 should be comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Guanidine HCl 7 M 95.53 29.4 g 3 Ethanol 56 % - 56 mL 4 H2O - - 44 mL

steps.title: The next steps depend on the intended throughput
steps.instruction: The next steps depend on the intended throughput: either single sample spin-columns or 96-well spin-column plates.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight (12-16...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight (12-16 hours) 37 °C, Increase to 55 oC for shorter digestion times

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 x g for 2 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 x g for 2 min.

steps.title: Add all of the sample solution...
steps.instruction: Add all of the sample solution (~ 600 μL) to a well in a 96-well silica membrane spin column ( we use SD5005 from NBS Biologicals ) Ensure there is a suitable collection tube beneath into which the flow-through will go.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute and discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 1 to each spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 1 to each spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute and discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 2 to each spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 2 to each spin column.

steps.title: Wash Buffer 2 should be pH...
steps.instruction: Wash Buffer 2 should be pH ~7 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Ethanol (100 %) 70 % 58.44 70 mL 3 1 M Tris-Hcl 10 mM - 1 mL 4 H2O - - 29 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Discard the collection tube and replace...
steps.instruction: Discard the collection tube and replace it with a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 100 - 200 μL Elution...
steps.instruction: Add 100 - 200 μL Elution Buffer directly to the silica membrane and leave it at room temperature for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Elution Buffer should be pH ~7...
steps.instruction: Elution Buffer should be pH ~7 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 1 M Tris-Hcl 10 mM - 1 mL 3 H2O - - 99 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute. The DNA is now in the collection tube and can be taken forward to amplification. Steps 26-27 can be repeated for increased DNA yield but a lower overall concentration. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation (e.g., 5 minutes) will work, although should not be necessary for this step.

steps.title: Repeat until a sufficient sample size has been collected
steps.instruction: Repeat until a sufficient sample size has been collected.

steps.title: Place all sample tubes from a...
steps.instruction: Place all sample tubes from a session in a zip-lock bag and keep cold under dry ice or place in a portable freezer to freeze for transport.

steps.title: Grind the larvae is a tissue homogeniser until homogenised
steps.instruction: Grind the larvae is a tissue homogeniser until homogenised. We use a Geno/Grinder 2010 at full speed (1750 RPM) for 2 minutes but different machines or even tube sizes and sample volumes will require separate optimised settings.

steps.title: To the lysate, add 1 mL...
steps.instruction: To the lysate, add 1 mL of freshly-prepared Proteinase Buffer , a master mix of Lysis Buffer 2 and Proteinase K (detailed in the sub-steps below) and vortex to mix. Note step from 4: if you have used different tube sizes and Lysis Buffer 1 volume from the one listed for 5 ml screwcap tubes, the volume of Proteinase Buffer needs to be adjusted proportionally so that it is always 1/3 of the Lysis Buffer 1 volume.

steps.title: Lysis Buffer 2 should be pH...
steps.instruction: Lysis Buffer 2 should be pH 9 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Sodium chloride 120 mM 58.44 0.701 g 3 1 M Tris-Hcl 50 mM - 5 mL 4 0.5 M EDTA 20 mM - 4 mL 5 SDS 3 % - 3 g 6 H2O - - 91 mL

steps.title: Per sample, the Proteinase Buffer master mix should comprise
steps.instruction: Per sample, the Proteinase Buffer master mix should comprise: A B 1 Reagent Amount per sample 2 Lysis solution 2 970 μL 3 Proteinase K (10 mg/mL) 30 μL

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 x g for 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 x g for 4 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.5 mL of the supernatant to a clean 2 mL screw-cap tube for archiving/backup.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Transfer 200 μL of the supernatant to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 μL of the supernatant to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. The remaining lysate can now be stored at -20 °C for backup/future work.

steps.title: Add 400 μL of master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μL of master mix of Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol (detailed below) to each sample.

steps.title: Protein Denaturation Buffer should be comprised...
steps.instruction: Protein Denaturation Buffer should be comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Guanidine HCl 5 M 95.53 47.7 g 3 H2O - - 100 mL

steps.title: Per 96-well plate, the Protein Denaturation...
steps.instruction: Per 96-well plate, the Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol master mix should comprise: A B C 1 Reagent Amount per sample Amount per sample 2 Protein Denaturation Buffer 220 μL 220 μL 3 Ethanol (100 %) 220 μL 220 μL

steps.title: Add 400 μL of master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μL of master mix of Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol (detailed below) to each well of a new 2.2 ml 96-well plate.

steps.title: Protein Denaturation Buffer should be comprised...
steps.instruction: Protein Denaturation Buffer should be comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Guanidine HCl 5 M 95.53 47.7 g 3 H2O - - 100 mL

steps.title: Add 200 μL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of the supernatant from STEP 10 into each well containing Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol.

steps.title: Per 96-well plate, the Protein Denaturation...
steps.instruction: Per 96-well plate, the Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol master mix should comprise: A B C 1 Reagent Amount per sample Amount per 96-well plate 2 Protein Denaturation Buffer 220 μL 42.2 mL 3 Ethanol (100 %) 220 μL 42.2 μL

steps.title: Add all of the sample solution...
steps.instruction: Add all of the sample solution (~ 600 μL) to a well in a 96-well DNA membrane spin column and cover with a breathable seal. Ensure there is a suitable reservoir beneath into which the flow-through will go (e.g., 2.2 mL deep-well plate).

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute and discard the flow-through. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation (e.g., 10 minutes) will work.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 1...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 1 to each spin column and cover with a breathable seal.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute and discard the flow-through. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation (e.g., 5 minutes) will work.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 2...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 2 to each spin column and cover with a breathable seal.

steps.title: Wash Buffer 2 should be pH...
steps.instruction: Wash Buffer 2 should be pH ~7 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Ethanol (100 %) 70 % 58.44 70 mL 3 1 M Tris-Hcl 10 mM - 1 mL 4 H2O - - 29 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 3 minutes and discard the flow-through. If the centrifuge cannot reach 20,000 x g , a longer centrifugation (e.g., 15 minutes) will work.

steps.title: Carefully move the spin column plate to a new 0
steps.instruction: Carefully move the spin column plate to a new 0.5 mL DNA collection plate. The liquid level following the final wash will be close to the base of the spin column, so take care not to let it touch to prevent ethanol transfer to the soon-to-be eluted DNA.

steps.title: Add 200 μL Elution Buffer directly...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL Elution Buffer directly to the silica membrane and leave it at room temperature for 5 minutes, covering with a breathable seal.

steps.title: Elution Buffer should be pH ~7...
steps.instruction: Elution Buffer should be pH ~7 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 1 M Tris-Hcl 10 mM - 1 mL 3 H2O - - 99 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 1 minute. The DNA is now in the collection plate and can be taken forward to amplification. Steps 20-21 can be repeated for increased DNA yield but a lower overall concentration. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation (e.g., 5 minutes) will work, although should not be necessary for this step.

steps.title: When ready to begin tissue digestion,...
steps.instruction: When ready to begin tissue digestion, defrost the tubes containing dead larvae in Lysis Buffer 1 .

steps.title: When ready to begin tissue digestion,...
steps.instruction: When ready to begin tissue digestion, defrost the tubes containing dead larvae in Lysis Buffer 1 .

steps.title: Add 400 μL of master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μL of master mix of Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol (detailed in the sub-step below) to each well of the 2.2 mL 96-well plate.

steps.title: Protein Denaturation Buffer should be comprised...
steps.instruction: Protein Denaturation Buffer should be comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Guanidine HCl 5 M 95.53 47.7 g 3 H2O - - 100 mL

steps.title: Per 96-well plate, the Protein Denaturation...
steps.instruction: Per 96-well plate, the Protein Denaturation Buffer and ethanol master mix should comprise: A B C 1 Reagent Amount per sample Amount per 96-well plate 2 Protein Denaturation Buffer 220 μL 21.1 mL 3 Ethanol (100 %) 220 μL 21.1 μL

steps.title: Add all of the sample solution...
steps.instruction: Add all of the sample solution (~ 600 μL) to a well in a 96-well silica membrane spin column ( we use SD5007 from NBS Biologicals ) and cover with a breathable seal . Ensure there is a suitable reservoir beneath into which the flow-through will go (e.g., 2.2 mL deep-well plate).

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 10...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 10 minutes and discard the flow-through. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation will work.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 1...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 1 to each spin column and cover with a breathable seal.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 5 minutes and discard the flow-through. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation will work.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 2...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Wash Buffer 2 to each spin column and cover with a breathable seal.

steps.title: Carefully move the spin column plate to a new 0
steps.instruction: Carefully move the spin column plate to a new 0.5 mL DNA collection plate. The liquid level following the final wash will be close to the base of the spin column, so take care not to let it touch to prevent ethanol transfer to the soon-to-be eluted DNA.

steps.title: Wash Buffer 2 should be pH...
steps.instruction: Wash Buffer 2 should be pH ~7 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Ethanol (100 %) 70 % 58.44 70 mL 3 1 M Tris-Hcl 10 mM - 1 mL 4 H2O - - 29 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 15...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 15 minutes and discard the flow-through. If the centrifuge cannot reach 20,000 x g , a longer centrifugation will work.

steps.title: Add 200 μL Elution Buffer directly...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL Elution Buffer directly to the silica membrane and leave it at room temperature for 5 minutes, covering with a breathable seal.

steps.title: Elution Buffer should be pH ~7...
steps.instruction: Elution Buffer should be pH ~7 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 1 M Tris-Hcl 10 mM - 1 mL 3 H2O - - 99 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 6000 x g for 2 minutes. The DNA is now in the collection plate and can be taken forward to amplification. Steps 26 - 27 can be repeated for increased DNA yield but a lower overall concentration. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6000 x g , a longer centrifugation (e.g., 5 minutes) will work, although should not be necessary for this step.

steps.title: Grind the larvae is a tissue homogeniser until homogenised
steps.instruction: Grind the larvae is a tissue homogeniser until homogenised. We use a Geno/Grinder 2010 at full speed (1750 RPM) for 2 minutes but different machines or even tube sizes and sample volumes will require separate optimised settings.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 x g for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 x g for 2 minutes.

steps.title: To the lysate, add 1 mL...
steps.instruction: To the lysate, add 1 mL of freshly-prepared Proteinase Buffer , a master mix of Lysis Buffer 2 and Proteinase K (detailed in the sub-steps below) and vortex to mix. Note step from 4: if you have used different tube sizes and Lysis Buffer 1 volume from the one listed for 5 ml screwcap tubes, the volume of Proteinase Buffer needs to be adjusted proportionally so that it is always 1/3 of the Lysis Buffer 1 volume.

steps.title: Lysis Buffer 2 should be pH...
steps.instruction: Lysis Buffer 2 should be pH 9 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Sodium chloride 120 mM 58.44 0.701 g 3 1 M Tris-Hcl 50 mM - 5 mL 4 0.5 M EDTA 20 mM - 4 mL 5 SDS 3 % - 3 g 6 H2O - - 91 mL

steps.title: Per plate, the Proteinase Buffer master mix should comprise
steps.instruction: Per plate, the Proteinase Buffer master mix should comprise: A B 1 Reagent Amount per 96-well plate 2 Lysis solution 2 102.4 mL 3 Proteinase K (10 mg/mL) 3.168 mL

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight (12-16...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight (12-16 hours) 37 °C, Increase to 55 oC for shorter digestion times

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 x g for 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 x g for 4 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer 1 mL of the supernatant to a clean 2
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 mL of the supernatant to a clean 2.2 mL deep-well 96-well plate for archiving/backup and retain the rest in the original plate.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000 x g for 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000 x g for 4 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer 200 μL of the supernatant to a clean 2
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 μL of the supernatant to a clean 2.2 mL deep-well 96-well plate. The remaining lysate can now be stored at -20 °C for backup/future work.

steps.title: Using sterile disposable forceps, place individual...
steps.instruction: Using sterile disposable forceps, place individual caterpilars into the pre-prepared collection tubes containing the hardened steel ball bearings and Lysis Buffer 1 . Be sure to label the tube with a sample number and any additional detail necessary for the study (e.g., processionary number). Alternatively barcode your tubes with preprinted labels and use data collection software such as epicollect5 to record sample IDs and metadata.

steps.title: Prepare Lysis Buffer 1
steps.instruction: Prepare Lysis Buffer 1. This should be pH 9 and comprised of the following reagents: A B C D 1 Reagent Required concentration in buffer Chemical molarity Amount per 100 mL 2 Sodium chloride 120 mM 58.44 0.701 g 3 1 M Tris-Hcl 50 mM - 5 mL 4 0.5 M EDTA 20 mM - 4 mL 5 H2O - - 91 mL See the next step for advice on buffer volumes you will need to prepare.

steps.title: Prepare 5 ml screwcap collection tubes...
steps.instruction: Prepare 5 ml screwcap collection tubes containing one 4 or 4.5 mm hardened steel bead and 2ml of Lysis Buffer 1. All volumes for sample and homogenisation and digestion assume you are using a 5 ml screwcap tube. See notes below for further detail. Note on tube and bead selection: Tube sizes, bead sizes and buffer volumes are all dependant on individual caterpillar size. For medium-sized caterpillars (e.g. Noctua pronuba or Pieris brassicae) we use 5 ml screwcap eppendorf tubes ( # 0030122313 ) with one 4 or 4.5 mm steel bead and 2 ml of Lysis Buffer 1 . For smaller caterpillars (e.g. leaf miners) use 2 ml screwcap tubes and two 3 mm beads and for larger caterpillars (e.g. hawkmoth caterpillars) you may need to use 15 ml or even 50 ml tubes with larger or multiple steel beads*. *When using tubes smaller or larger than 5 ml scale volumes of Lysis Buffer 1 , Lysis Buffer 2 and Proteinase K throughout to match tube volume. Note on steel bead material choice: If you are extracting already collected caterpillars in the lab for immediate DNA extraction, you can use the cheaper carbon steel ball bearings. If you plan to use pre-prepared tubes for direct collection of caterpillars into lysis buffer in the field*, use stainless steel bearings to prevent them from rusting. *Caterpillars stored in Lysis Buffer 1 at -20 o C are extractable at least 6 months after collection.

steps.title: Beads are usually shipped coated in...
steps.instruction: Beads are usually shipped coated in manufacturing oil (especially the carbon steel beads). To remove this, place beads in a borosilicate glass beaker or Duran bottle with the pouring lip and lid removed then bake for at least 12 hours at 250 o C. Figure 1: Depending on baking time, carbon steel beads will change colour, this is normal. Baked beads should be stored in a closed airtight DNA free container until needed, do not touch with bare hands to prevent contamination.

steps.title: Source steel beads (ball bearings) for...
steps.instruction: Source steel beads (ball bearings) for tissue grinding (Tungsten beads are not usually necessary for caterpillars). We use hardened carbon steel or stainless steel bearings from simplybearings.co.uk . This protocol requires one bead per sample tube.

steps.title: Note on centrifugation times
steps.instruction: Note on centrifugation times: All centrifugation times and speeds below are based on a Megafuge 40R *. This centrifuge cannot reach the same speeds as the Qiagen Centrifuge 4-16 . If you have this faster centrifuge, then refer to the Qiagen DNAeasy 96 protocol for speeds and times. *The benefit of the Megafuge 40R over the Qiagen 4-16 is that despite being slightly slower, it can process four 96 well plates at once compared to two for the Qiagen 4-16.
